21.correct: The Chief Justice of India is appointed by the President of India.

wrong.B: The Prime Minister advises but does not formally appoint the CJI.
wrong.C: The Vice-President has no role in appointing the CJI.
wrong.D: The Law Minister does not appoint the CJI.

21.fact: By convention (and the Collegium system), the senior-most Supreme Court judge is appointed CJI.

22.correct: At the Union level, the Council of Ministers is collectively responsible to the Lok Sabha (the popularly elected House).

wrong.A: It is appointed by the President but answerable to the Lok Sabha.
wrong.B: It is not responsible to the Supreme Court.
wrong.D: It is not collectively responsible to the Rajya Sabha.

22.fact: If the Lok Sabha passes a no-confidence motion, the whole Council of Ministers must resign.

23.correct: An AQI value of 0-50 represents 'Good' air quality in India's Air Quality Index.
23.fact: The AQI ranges from 0 to 500; higher values mean worse air quality.

24.correct: In the Human Development Index, the education dimension is measured using mean years of schooling and expected years of schooling.

wrong.B: Enrolment and dropout ratios are not the HDI education measures.
wrong.C: Literacy rate and school enrolment were used in the older HDI, but the current measure uses mean and expected years of schooling.
wrong.D: Adult literacy and teacher-student ratio are not the HDI education indicators.

24.fact: The HDI combines education, income (GNI per capita) and life expectancy.

25.correct: Bahlul Lodi founded the Lodi dynasty, the last ruling house of the Delhi Sultanate.

wrong.A: Sikandar Lodi was Bahlul's son and successor.
wrong.B: Ibrahim Lodi was the last Lodi sultan, defeated at Panipat (1526).

25.fact: Ibrahim Lodi's defeat by Babur at the First Battle of Panipat (1526) began Mughal rule.

26.correct: The Simon Commission (1927) was boycotted because it had no Indian members - it was an all-British commission deciding India's constitutional future.

wrong.B: It was not boycotted for recommending provincial autonomy.
wrong.C: It did not propose independent colony status.
wrong.D: It did not abolish separate electorates.

26.fact: Protesters greeted it with 'Simon Go Back'; Lala Lajpat Rai was injured in a related protest.

27.correct: A market economy is also known as capitalism, where production and prices are determined mainly by private decisions and market forces.

wrong.A: Communism is a system of collective/state ownership.
wrong.B: Feudalism is a medieval land-based hierarchy.
wrong.D: Socialism emphasises state/collective ownership and planning.

27.fact: India follows a 'mixed economy', combining elements of capitalism and socialism.

28.correct: In the aerobic breakdown of pyruvate inside the mitochondria, the products are carbon dioxide, water and energy (ATP).

wrong.A: Lactic acid forms in anaerobic respiration in muscles, not aerobic breakdown.
wrong.C: Ethanol and CO2 form in anaerobic respiration in yeast (fermentation).
wrong.D: Lactic acid with CO2 is not the aerobic product.

28.fact: Aerobic respiration yields far more ATP per glucose molecule than anaerobic respiration.

29.correct: The theme of the Swachhata Hi Seva 2024 campaign was 'Swabhav Swachhata, Sanskar Swachhata' (cleanliness as habit and value).

wrong.B: 'Swachhata se Samriddhi' was not the 2024 theme.
wrong.C: 'Plastic-Free India' was not the campaign theme.
wrong.D: 'Har Ghar Swachhata' was not the 2024 theme.

29.fact: Swachhata Hi Seva is held annually around Gandhi Jayanti to promote cleanliness.

30.correct: Madurai was the capital of the Pandya kings of ancient Tamil Nadu.

wrong.C: Amaravati was associated with the Satavahanas.

30.fact: Madurai's Meenakshi Amman Temple is one of the most famous temples in South India.

31.correct: Forests (I) are in the Concurrent List (added by the 42nd Amendment). Banking (II) is in the Union List, not the Concurrent List - so only I is correct.

wrong.B: 'Only II' is wrong; Banking is a Union List subject.
wrong.C: 'Both' is wrong because Banking is not on the Concurrent List.
wrong.D: 'Neither' is wrong since Forests are on the Concurrent List.

31.fact: The Concurrent List also includes subjects like education, marriage and criminal law.

32.correct: In Mizoram, 'Chapchar Kut' is celebrated as a thanksgiving festival after clearing forests for jhum (shifting) cultivation.

wrong.A: 'Dree' is a festival of the Apatani tribe of Arunachal Pradesh.
wrong.D: 'Behdienkhlam' is a festival of the Jaintia tribe of Meghalaya.

32.fact: Chapchar Kut is a spring festival marked by the colourful Cheraw (bamboo) dance.

33.correct: The National Rainfed Area Authority functions under the Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare.

wrong.A: It is not under the Ministry of Cooperation.
wrong.B: It is not under the Ministry of Jal Shakti.
wrong.D: It is not under the Ministry of Earth Sciences.

33.fact: It coordinates the development of India's large rain-fed (non-irrigated) farming areas.

34.correct: The primary function of a hearing aid is to amplify sound so that it can be heard more clearly by a person with hearing loss.

wrong.A: It does not merely reduce noise.
wrong.B: It does not convert sound into light signals.
wrong.D: It does not change the frequency of sound.

34.fact: A hearing aid has a microphone, an amplifier and a speaker.

35.correct: As per Census 2011, Rajasthan - India's largest state by area - holds only about 5.5% of the country's total population.
35.fact: Uttar Pradesh is India's most populous state, with about 16-17% of the population.

36.correct: Open Market Operations refer to the buying and selling of government bonds/securities by the RBI in the open market to control the money supply.

wrong.A: Moral suasion is persuasion, not bond buying/selling.
wrong.B: 'Margin requirement' relates to loan collateral, not open-market bond trades.
wrong.D: 'Evergreening of loans' refers to extending fresh loans to hide bad debt.

36.fact: Selling bonds reduces money supply; buying bonds injects liquidity into the economy.

37.correct: Michael Phelps won a record 8 gold medals at the 2008 Beijing Olympics.

wrong.A: At Rio 2016 he won multiple golds but not a single-Games record of 8.
wrong.C: At London 2012 he did not win 8 golds.

37.fact: Phelps is the most decorated Olympian of all time with 23 gold medals overall.

38.correct: The Philadelphi Corridor is a strip located along the border between Gaza and Egypt.

wrong.A: It is not on the Iran border.
wrong.B: It is not on the Turkey border.
wrong.C: It is not on the UAE border.

38.fact: The Philadelphi Corridor is a narrow buffer zone along the Gaza-Egypt frontier.

39.correct: 'Annihilation of Caste' was written by Dr B.R. Ambedkar, a powerful critique of the caste system.

wrong.B: Dadabhai Naoroji wrote 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India'.
wrong.C: Tarak Nath Das was a revolutionary, not the author.
wrong.D: Chittaranjan Das was a freedom leader, not the author of this book.

39.fact: 'Annihilation of Caste' (1936) was originally a speech that was never delivered, later published as a book.

40.correct: Sania Mirza has never won an Olympic medal, whereas the others have.

wrong.A: P.V. Sindhu has won Olympic silver (2016) and bronze (2020).
wrong.C: Saina Nehwal won an Olympic bronze (2012).
wrong.D: Mary Kom won an Olympic bronze in boxing (2012).

40.fact: Sania Mirza is a multiple Grand Slam doubles champion but reached only the semi-finals at the Olympics.
